description_long: This object consists of four adjacent metal control cabinets, each with a rectangular form and flat surfaces, commonly found in urban environments for housing electrical or communication equipment. The cabinets are primarily light gray, with visible graffiti in black and green spray paint on the front panels, adding an urban, weathered appearance. Each cabinet features a door with a small handle and a visible lock mechanism, and several have yellow warning or information labels affixed near the top. The surfaces are smooth and metallic, with no visible ventilation grilles, and the cabinets are floor-standing, designed to be fixed in place. The tops and sides are plain, while the front faces are the most detailed, showing the doors, handles, locks, and graffiti. The overall structure is robust and utilitarian, suitable for outdoor installation on sidewalks or near roads.
description: A group of four gray metal control cabinets with graffiti, warning labels, and lockable doors, designed for urban utility use. The cabinets are floor-standing and have a weathered, functional appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the cabinets, with a small handle and graffiti visible on the end cabinet.
description_view_1: Front view displaying all four cabinet doors, graffiti, warning labels, and handles, highlighting the main interactive features.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the narrow profile and end cabinet details.
description_view_3: Rear view of the cabinets, showing plain surfaces without graffiti, labels, or handles.
